_comment: Raw-HCI Coded-PHY-only backend — for Nordic SoftDevice-Controller firmware (e.g. nRF52840 dongle running Zephyr hci_usb).
_comment_why: SDC cannot scan 1M+Coded together (HCI 0x11), which is what the Linux kernel always requests, so BlueZ-based scanning never starts. This backend drives the controller over a raw HCI_CHANNEL_USER socket and scans LE Coded PHY only (Scanning_PHYs=0x04). Requires CAP_NET_RAW+CAP_NET_ADMIN (granted by the shipped systemd unit); no external tools needed (uses the HCIDEVDOWN/HCIDEVUP ioctls, not btmgmt).
_comment_scan_backend: bluez | hci_coded | auto (default auto). Use hci_coded explicitly for SDC firmware; auto falls back to it on the BlueZ multi-PHY rejection pattern.
_comment_hci_coded: dev_id defaults from bluetooth_adapter (hci0 -> 0). interval/window are in 0.625ms units (96 = 60ms), window <= interval. random_address must be a static random address (top two bits of the first octet set).
_comment_filters: Software whitelists apply to every backend. The hci_coded backend has no hardware UUID filter, so filtering happens in software here.
